사무엘하 1:1.content: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had stayed two days in Ziklag;

사무엘하 1:2.content: on the third day, behold, a man came out of the camp from Saul, with his clothes torn, and earth on his head. When he came to David, he fell to the earth, and showed respect.

사무엘하 1:3.content: David said to him, “Where do you come from?” He said to him, “I have escaped out of the camp of Israel.”

사무엘하 1:4.content: David said to him, “How did it go? Please tell me.” He answered, “The people have fled from the battle, and many of the people also have fallen and are dead. Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.”

사무엘하 1:5.content: David said to the young man who told him, “How do you know that Saul and Jonathan his son are dead?”

사무엘하 1:6.content: The young man who told him said, “As I happened by chance on Mount Gilboa, behold, Saul was leaning on his spear; and behold, the chariots and the horsemen followed close behind him.

사무엘하 1:7.content: When he looked behind him, he saw me, and called to me. I answered, ‘Here I am.’

사무엘하 1:8.content: He said to me, ‘Who are you?’ I answered him, ‘I am an Amalekite.’

사무엘하 1:9.content: He said to me, ‘Please stand beside me, and kill me; for anguish has taken hold of me, because my life lingers in me.’

사무엘하 1:10.content: So I stood beside him and killed him, because I was sure that he could not live after he had fallen. I took the crown that was on his head and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them here to my lord.”

사무엘하 1:11.content: Then David took hold on his clothes, and tore them; and all the men who were with him did likewise.

사무엘하 1:12.content: They mourned, wept, and fasted until evening, for Saul, and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of Yahweh, and for the house of Israel; because they had fallen by the sword.

사무엘하 1:13.content: David said to the young man who told him, “Where are you from?” He answered, “I am the son of a foreigner, an Amalekite.”

사무엘하 1:14.content: David said to him, “Why were you not afraid to stretch out your hand to destroy Yahweh’s anointed?”

사무엘하 1:15.content: David called one of the young men, and said, “Go near, and cut him down!” He struck him so that he died.

사무엘하 1:16.content: David said to him, “Your blood be on your head; for your mouth has testified against you, saying, ‘I have slain Yahweh’s anointed.’ ”

사무엘하 1:17.content: David lamented with this lamentation over Saul and over Jonathan his son

사무엘하 1:18.content: (and he commanded them to teach the children of Judah the song of the bow; behold, it is written in the book of Jashar):

사무엘하 1:19.content: “Your glory, Israel, was slain on your high places! How the mighty have fallen!

사무엘하 1:20.content: Don’t tell it in Gath. Don’t publish it in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.

사무엘하 1:21.content: You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain on you, and no fields of offerings; For there the shield of the mighty was defiled and cast away, The shield of Saul was not anointed with oil.

사무엘하 1:22.content: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, Jonathan’s bow didn’t turn back. Saul’s sword didn’t return empty.

사무엘하 1:23.content: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives. In their death, they were not divided. They were swifter than eagles. They were stronger than lions.

사무엘하 1:24.content: You daughters of Israel, weep over Saul, who clothed you delicately in scarlet, who put ornaments of gold on your clothing.

사무엘하 1:25.content: How the mighty have fallen in the middle of the battle! Jonathan was slain on your high places.

사무엘하 1:26.content: I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan. You have been very pleasant to me. Your love to me was wonderful, passing the love of women.

사무엘하 1:27.content: How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war have perished!”
